description:
Goal: derive a backend-owned executable-plan projection from Compiler2 materialized closed artifacts, without reopening semantic discovery or depending on old-world planner fallback.

Signal: each materialized executable carries enough settled backend facts for downstream adapters to consume mechanically: pruned body shape, selected call edges, per-value or per-slot type/representation facts, callable-target facts, extern marshal decisions, and allocation/effect facts. No backend adapter reaches back into semantic analysis or asks the old planner/interpreter to infer missing facts.

Strategy: move the missing backend-facing semantic results into Compiler2-owned facts first, then have materialization project those facts into a backend-owned program surface. Build the artifact data model around what backends actually need, not around the current semantic-internal maps.

Change:
- Identify and publish the missing semantic facts that today die inside AnalyzeActivation but that backend work needs later.
- Add a Compiler2 artifact-side executable-plan/program model that contains the closed backend surface, not just lowered bodies plus call edges.
- Update materialization to project only from SemanticClosed plus those owned semantic facts.
- Add tests proving the artifact projection is sufficient and that no backend path falls back to semantic/type/reachability recomputation.

Why:
- Current MaterializedProgram is an honest closure snapshot, but it is not yet a backend-ready contract. It lacks the settled executable-plan facts the old interpreter/JIT/AOT path expects, so wiring adapters directly to it would either rediscover semantics or smuggle in old fallback behavior.

Checks:
- Artifact projection tests pin the quicksort and Enum.reduce closed frontiers plus the new backend-facing facts.
- Any attempted backend fallback to type/reachability/callee discovery is fatal.
- The resulting artifact surface is stable enough for fz-rh2.8.2 to derive AbiReady/Emitted mechanically.
title: Compiler2 artifacts: project backend executable plans from closed facts
